algorithms.title: Resuscitation Team Roles
algorithms.description: Typical defined roles around an ACLS resuscitation, adaptable to team size.
steps: Team leader: directs the resuscitation, interprets rhythms, assigns tasks by name, maintains the overall plan.
Compressor(s): deliver high-quality compressions, rotate ~every 2 minutes.
Airway/ventilation: manages BVM or advanced airway, delivers breaths at the correct rate.
Medication/IV-IO: establishes access, prepares and administers drugs, confirms doses aloud.
CPR coach / quality monitor: watches rate, depth, recoil, and pause duration; gives real-time feedback.
Recorder/timer: documents times, rhythms, and interventions; tracks the 2-minute cycle.
All roles: use closed-loop communication and speak up via constructive intervention when needed.

algorithms.title: Ventilation Strategy Transition
algorithms.description: How ventilation changes the moment an advanced airway is confirmed in place.
steps: Before advanced airway: 30 compressions : 2 breaths, minimizing pause for breaths.
Advanced airway (SGA or ETT) placed and position confirmed.
Switch to continuous compressions with no pause for ventilation.
Deliver 1 breath every 6 seconds (~10/min), avoiding hyperventilation.
Reassess capnography and compression quality together every ~2 minutes.
